data.title: Ideal Power signals initial revenue ramp in H2 2025 as B-TRAN secures new global partnerships
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ideal Power Inc. (IPWR) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Dan Brdar, President and CEO, highlighted the completion of solid-state circuit breaker prototypes for the first design win, delivered three months ahead of schedule, and noted ongoing collaboration with the customer for product introduction in the coming months. He stated, "We expect solid-state circuit breaker customers, including our first design win customer, to drive our initial revenue ramp starting in the second half of this year."
  • Brdar announced internal approval at Stellantis for a new EV contactor program, which is advancing through their purchase order approval process, representing a potential second program with Stellantis in addition to the drivetrain inverter initiative. He added, "This program would meaningfully accelerate our timeline to substantial product revenue from our relationship with Stellantis."
  • The company secured an order from a third Forbes Global 500 power management market leader, bringing the total to three such leaders evaluating the technology for solid-state circuit breakers. Brdar said, "Design wins with any of these companies could lead to several million dollars or more of annual revenue for us."
  • Sekorm, a demand creation distributor, secured an order from a new customer focused on solid-state circuit breaker applications, and the company shipped evaluation boards to several potential design win customers, indicating growing momentum.
  • A new sales partnership was established with Queensland Semiconductor Technologies (Quest Semi), expanding reach into Europe and Asia and aligning with Quest Semi's presence in electric vehicles, renewable energy, and industrial motor drives.
  • Brdar emphasized industry demand, noting, "There’s clearly growing awareness and interest in our technology for solid-state circuit protection, both for industrial and utility applications and EV contactors," and positioned B-TRAN as the enabling technology for widespread adoption.
  • Tim Burns, CFO, stated, "Our first quarter 2025 cash burn was $2.1 million up from $2 million in the first quarter of 2024 and down significantly from the $2.6 million cash burn in the fourth quarter of 2024. Our Q1 cash burn was below our guidance of $2.2 million to $2.4 million due to the timing of spending and related payments."

Outlook

  • Brdar projected commercial sales from the initial solid-state circuit breaker product later this year and expects revenue of several hundred thousand dollars in the first year, with the opportunity to exceed $1 million in the second year from the first design win customer. He shared, "We expect initial revenue ramp beginning in the second half of this year."
  • Burns indicated, "We expect second quarter 2025 cash burn to increase to approximately $2.5 million to $2.7 million with the full year 2025 cash burn over $10 million with the increase primarily due to 2024 and planned 2025 hiring."
  • The company anticipates modest commercial revenue in Q2 2025, with the main ramp starting in H2 2025.

Financial Results

  • CFO Burns reported, "Cash and cash equivalents totaled $13.7 million at March 31, 2025. We have no debt in a clean capital structure. We recorded modest revenue for the first quarter of 2025 as customers continue to evaluate our technology."
  • Operating expenses were $2.8 million in Q1 2025, compared to $2.5 million in Q1 2024.
  • Net loss in Q1 2025 was $2.7 million compared to $2.5 million in Q1 2024.

Q&A

  • Casey Ryan, WestPark Capital, inquired about Sekorm's order fulfillment and related revenue timing. Burns explained, "That order, we just started shipping, it's partially fulfilled right now... some of the shipments were in Q1... some are coming in Q2, Q3."
  • Ryan asked about the Stellantis purchase order process timeline. Brdar responded, "It will probably take several weeks to get through that process, but it's not something that we're going to be looking at a couple of quarters from now still waiting for."
  • Ryan questioned the long-term opportunity with Stellantis. Brdar clarified, "We won't be providing the contactor or the drivetrain. We'll be providing the semiconductors... for us, the win gets you into multiple vehicles and multiple brands in Stellantis."
  • Ryan sought clarification on other OEM automotive opportunities. Burns confirmed, "There's a second opportunity with a large global automaker that's specific to EV contactors... and then there is a third automaker that we are still engaged with."
  • A webcast question asked about Wolfspeed's financial challenges. Brdar stated, "It really highlights why we are so focused on an asset-light business model... not only are we fabless, we'll never own a fab."
  • On tariffs and supply chain, Brdar said, "Power semiconductors are exempt from most tariffs currently in effect... there's really no tariff impact for us."
  • Questions covered SymCool pipeline, customer attrition (none reported), sales cycle (roughly 12 months for industrial customers), and cash flow breakeven (a few key design wins needed).
  • On auto certification, Brdar projected, "We're targeting the end of this year, sometime in the fourth quarter to get it done."
  • Burns estimated Ideal Power technology content in EVs at "roughly $1,100 per vehicle."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive tone, focusing on commercialization timing, partnership expansion, and scalability of opportunities.
  • Management's tone was confident and forward-looking during prepared remarks, frequently referencing expected milestones and revenue ramp. In the Q&A, management remained consistent, providing detailed, direct answers without signs of defensiveness or hesitation.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment remains optimistic but with greater emphasis on execution and upcoming revenue realization.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter added a third Forbes Global 500 customer and formalized a new international sales partnership, while the previous quarter focused more on pipeline expansion and program reviews.
  • Guidance language sharpened this quarter, with specific revenue ramp expectations for H2 2025 and additional detail on customer engagement outcomes.
  • Management confidence is consistent with last quarter, but there is increased specificity regarding timelines and order fulfillment.
  • Analysts' questions in both quarters centered on commercialization progress and customer traction, with the current call reflecting heightened interest in near-term revenue and design win conversion.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management addressed tariff and supply chain risks, stating, "Power semiconductors are exempt from most tariffs currently in effect... we remain focused on our execution."
  • Cash burn is expected to increase due to hiring, with full-year cash burn projected over $10 million.
  • The sales cycle for large customers remains lengthy and variable, though management indicated no loss of engaged customers.
  • Automotive certification remains a key milestone, with completion targeted by year-end.

Final Takeaway

Ideal Power's management emphasized accelerating commercialization milestones, including a growing roster of major global partners, early design wins, and new program approvals with Stellantis and other automakers. The company projects its initial revenue ramp to begin in the second half of 2025, supported by expanding customer engagements, a robust patent estate, and a resilient asset-light model designed to mitigate supply chain risks. Management anticipates several key commercial announcements and remains focused on executing its growth strategy across industrial and automotive applications.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Bayer exec: Trump pharma tariffs would negatively impact R&D
data.description:
If President Trump's threat to impose tariffs on pharmaceutical products goes through, it would have severe consequences for industry R&D efforts, according to a Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) pharma executive. 

"Every dollar we spend on tariffs is a dollar we can’t spend on research and development," Bayer U.S. President and Pharmaceuticals COO Sebastian Guth said Thursday at the FT Live US Pharma and Biotech Summit in New York City.

The exec also criticized the Executive Order signed on May 15 by President Trump that aims to lower prescription drug prices through the "Most Favored Nation program," arguing it won't do much to help patients. However, like Trump and Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) CEO Christopher Boerner who spoke earlier in the morning at the summit, Guth said that drug middlemen -- pharmacy benefit managers -- share much of the blame for high drug prices in the U.S.

The exec noted that while Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) isn't forecasting an impact from potential tariffs in 2025, in 2026 and beyond "we are preparing to ensure we have continued robust supply chains."

Other pharmas are also following suit, according to Rebecca Wood, former FDA chief counsel and currently a partner at Sidney Austin. She said that companies are looking at foreign trade zones, on shoring activities, renegotiating supply agreements, and diversifying where supplies are sourced from. 

Guth noted that over the last decade, R&D spending by pharmas in the U.S. has drastically increased so much so that it now greatly outpaces what is done in Europe. Americans also have access to more innovative medicines compared to Europeans, and they get them faster, he added.

Tim Hunt, CEO of the Alliance for Regenerative Medicine, said he was recently in Europe and noted that there is a renewed effort to drive pharma innovation there.

Hunt noted he and others have met with HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to discuss ways to improve deregulation and reforms at the agency. He said RFK Jr. was receptive to ideas, but cautioned both wanted "common sense reforms" so "we don't become like the Wild Wild West."

Guth added he also met with FDA Commissioner Marty Makary, and they discussed accelerating drug development and the use of more technology to improve the work at the agency.

data.title: Virtu Financial stock slides after BofA downgrades to Neutral on valuation
data.description:
Virtu Financial (NASDAQ:VIRT) shares shed 5.1% in Thursday late morning trading after Bank of America Securities downgraded the stock to Neutral from Buy, given its recent outperformance, and, consequently, less attractive valuation.

Shares of the electronic market maker jumped 75% from a year ago and some 14% year-to-date, as the company recorded robust volumes in its Market Making and Execution Services units, benefiting from broad market volatility. That compares with the S&P 500's YTD and Y/Y returns of 0.2% and 12%.

Seeking Alpha's Quant system gives VIRT a valuation grade of "C," with the poorest mark in price-to-book. 

Fundamentally, while Virtu (NASDAQ:VIRT) could post another strong quarter in Q2, "we have become more concerned that volumes/spreads could experience a meaningful decline in 2H25," analyst Craig Siegenthaler wrote in a note to clients.

He added that he is "particularly concerned with the retail channel where volumes could fade following investor losses YTD. Despite better market returns in recent weeks, we think online broker clients are still nursing YTD losses related to the March/April equity market drawdown."

BofA's Neutral rate differs from the SA Quant system rating of Strong Buy and the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.

data.title: I Squared confirms deal to buy stake in Matterhorn gas pipeline with Enbridge, MPLX
data.description:
I Squared Capital, together with Enbridge (NYSE:ENB) and MPLX (NYSE:MPLX), confirmed Thursday an agreement to buy a significant equity interest in the Matterhorn Express natural gas pipeline; the purchase price was not disclosed but speculation had said a deal could value Matterhorn at more than $5B including debt.

Under the deal terms, I Squared will acquire ~37% of Matterhorn through its ISQ Global Infrastructure Fund III, alongside infrastructure company WhiteWater Midstream and infrastructure investment firm FIC; following the close of the transaction, the pipeline will be owned 65% by MXP HoldCo, 15% by ONEOK (OKE), 10% by Enbridge (NYSE:ENB) and 10% by MPLX (NYSE:MPLX); WhiteWater Midstream, through MXP HoldCo, will continue to operate Matterhorn.

The 510-mile Matterhorn pipeline was placed into service in November 2024 and has ~2.5B cf/day of fully contracted capacity; it transports natural gas from the Waha area in the Permian Basin to Wharton, Texas, with additional delivery capabilities into the Katy, Texas area.

data.title: Wall Street rally loses some steam; Dow seesaws as Cisco surge offsets Walmart weakness
data.description:
Wall Street was muted on Thursday, as a combination of buyer exhaustion and some lackluster economic data weighed on sentiment. U.S. Treasury yields fell as traders snapped up bonds.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.05% at 5,889.59 points in morning trade, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.39% at 19,071.53 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.04% at 42,068.44 points.

A three-day rally sparked by a surprise trade deal between the U.S. and China and a benign consumer inflation report lost some steam. 

"The strong recent equity rally showed some signs of exhaustion over the past 24 hours, even though gains for the tech mega caps helped the S&P 500 (+0.10%) extend its gains since the closing low on April 7 to +18.26%," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said. 

Earlier, before the opening bell on Thursday, investors received a slew of economic indicators. First up was the April producer price index (PPI) report, with both headline and core producer prices unexpectedly declining on a M/M basis. 

At the same time, U.S. Census Bureau said retail sales in April edged up +0.1% M/M, just above the consensus for a flat reading. Core retail sales also increased by a similar amount, but came in below the expected rise.   

"Because it largely represents goods spending, which comprises less than a third of overall personal consumption in the United States, the retail sales report is not typically an A-lister among economic indicators…until now," Wells Fargo's Tim Quinlan said. 

"For the next few months at least, the retail sales report is set to be center stage as we seek to measure the impact of tariffs. While import duties can introduce pass-through effect to the service sector, it is goods that are apt to be the most impacted. We may eventually see these impacts manifested through product scarcity or higher prices, but the first action will simply be evident in the sales activity," Quinlan added. 

Additionally, two measures of manufacturing activity continued to show weakness in the sector. 

Also weighing on sentiment on Thursday was Walmart (WMT), -1.6%. The U.S. retailing giant's management on an earnings conference call warned that tariffs could lead to higher prices for consumers. 

Offsetting some of Walmart's (WMT) weakness was Cisco (CSCO). Shares of the networking giant were +6.7% after its solid quarterly results and guidance.  

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were lower. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was down 7 basis points to 4.47%, while the shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 6 basis points to 3.99%. 

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

data.title: Caesars added as a new long idea at Hedgeye
data.description:
Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR) was added as a new long idea at Hedgeye. 

"We think CZR bears are overstaying their welcome," Hedgeye analyst Sean Jenkins wrote in a note on Thursday. "Our work suggest consensus is: 1) too bearish on near-term GGR growth around the US; 2) much too bearish on CZR’s position within the regional & online gaming ecosystems; 3) ascribing little to no value to their omni-channel growth business."

Shares of Caesars (NASDAQ:CZR) fell 2% on Thursday despite the bullish note. Caesar's short interest is 13%.

"CZR appears cheap on numbers that could (finally) be moving higher," Jenkins added. "Cheap is not catalyst, but the rate-of-change in top line trends, EBITDA revisions, leverage, and shareholder returns certainly are (especially against a backdrop of multi-year highs in short interest)."
